channel.title: Dynamism on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Dynamism on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Objective-C Unrecognized Selector Sent to Instance — Runtime Error
item.description:
Unrecognized Selector Sent to Instance — Runtime Error

An unrecognized selector sent to instance error occurs when you try to call a method on an object that doesn’t implement it. The Objective-C runtime throws an NSInvalidArgumentException and terminates the application.

Description

Objective-C uses dynamic dispatch — method calls are resolved at runtime by looking up the selector (method name) in the object’s method table. If the object’s class (or any superclass) doesn’t implement the method, the runtime raises this exception. This is different from compile-time errors in statically typed languages.
